extension.summary: Stream All The Sources - 120+ scrapers, Stremio, Trakt, Debrid, Live Movie Channels
extension.description:
SALTS is a multi-source video addon with 120+ scrapers providing maximum coverage for movies and TV shows
NOTE (v2.9.21): All scrapers now live in a separate Kodi script module, "Salts Scrapers - Scrape All The Sources" (script.module.salts_scrapers), authored by Zeus768. It is auto-installed as a dependency.
extension.news: v2.9.69 (2026-05) - TORBOX FINAL FIX - STOPPED REPEATED API CALLS |- Fixed: The ACTUAL root cause - Kodi was calling torbox_play_file() multiple ||| times (4 calls in 18 seconds) because I was using player.play() instead of ||| xbmcplugin.setResolvedUrl(). Each retry made a NEW TorBox API request, ||| causing API spam that eventually kicked the stream out. ||| ||| THE REAL ISSUE ALL ALONG: ||| - Using player.play() with plugin:// URLs causes Kodi to retry on failure ||| - Each retry calls torbox_play_file() AGAIN ||| - Each call makes a NEW requestdl API call to TorBox ||| - Multiple rapid API calls → TorBox rate limits/kicks stream ||| ||| v2.9.69 Solution: ||| 1. Changed from player.play() to xbmcplugin.setResolvedUrl() ||| 2. setResolvedUrl tells Kodi "here's the final URL, stop calling me" ||| 3. Function only called ONCE, not 4+ times ||| 4. Single TorBox API request instead of spam ||| 5. Stream plays smoothly without repeated loading attempts ||| ||| This was the issue from the very first report - stream would start playing ||| but then keep trying to load and eventually fail. Now fixed properly. ||| v2.9.68 (2026-05) - TORBOX CODEC FIX - REMOVED ALL HEADERS |- Fixed: "CVideoPlayerVideo::OpenStream - could not open video codec" error. ||| v2.9.67 was adding headers using pipe syntax (URL|User-Agent=...) which was ||| preventing the video codec from opening properly. Kodi couldn't parse the ||| URL format correctly, causing immediate playback failure. ||| ||| ROOT CAUSE FOUND: Headers in pipe format break FFmpeg's video codec initialization. ||| ||| v2.9.68 Changes: ||| 1. REMOVED ALL headers from URL (no pipe syntax) ||| 2. Using CLEAN TorBox URL only ||| 3. Let Kodi/FFmpeg handle the connection natively ||| 4. Simplified torbox_play_file() function completely ||| ||| TorBox URLs work WITHOUT custom headers - they don't need User-Agent. ||| The previous failures were caused by my header additions, not lack of headers. ||| v2.9.67 (2026-05) - TORBOX PLAYBACK FIX - REMOVED INCOMPATIBLE INPUTSTREAM SETTINGS |- Fixed: TorBox streams now play immediately without long timeouts or failures. ||| v2.9.66 was forcing inputstream.adaptive with HLS manifest type, but TorBox ||| serves DIRECT MKV/MP4 files, not HLS streams. This mismatch caused Kodi to ||| wait ~80 seconds trying to parse the file as HLS before failing with ||| "Playlist Player: skipping unplayable item". ||| ||| Changes in v2.9.67: ||| 1. REMOVED inputstream.adaptive force (let Kodi auto-detect stream type) ||| 2. REMOVED HLS manifest_type setting (not applicable to direct files) ||| 3. REMOVED application/vnd.apple.mpegurl MIME type ||| 4. Simplified headers to just User-Agent (all that's needed) ||| 5. Kodi now correctly handles MKV/MP4 files with native FFmpeg ||| ||| All other v2.9.66 fixes retained: ||| - Fresh URL requests (no caching) ||| - Retry logic with exponential backoff ||| - Monitor pause during playback |||
